summary: First iteration E2E testing of CEMA cUAS operator console. Full backend suite (21 pytest cases) covers auth, detections CRUD + simulate + upload, CEMA/killchain advance, spectrum, MAVLink craft/broadcast/list, payload deploy (target + broadcast), mission logs and WebSocket (auth reject + live packet). ALL 21 backend tests passed. Frontend automation validated: /login redirect, RESTRICTED banner top/bottom, login with prefilled creds, dashboard stat tiles + waterfall + detections-table + SIM CONTACT increment, Signals 7-stage pipeline + cema-advance-btn, MAVLink hex-preview + broadcast-btn adding pkt-<id> to live stream via WS, 10 payload cards PL-001..PL-010, PL-005 deploy toast, KillChain kc-<id> nodes with NEUTRALIZED indicator, Mission Log populated with kinds AUTH/DETECTION/MAVLINK/PAYLOAD/CEMA/KILLCHAIN/UPLOAD, logout returning to /login. All acceptance criteria in the review request pass.

issues: React dev-time warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' — caused by an emergent dev tag-injector wrapping option text in a <span>. Not a functional bug; only appears in dev console. Consider suppressing or wrapping options with plain text nodes in prod builds.

Benign console warning 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established.' emitted when navigating away from /mavlink before the ws handshake completes; the cleanup path in useEffect is correct — could optionally guard ws.close() until readyState !== CONNECTING to silence the warning.

action_items: (Optional) Silence the '<span> child of <option>' dev warning by ensuring the dev tag injector does not wrap <option> text.
(Optional) Guard WebSocket cleanup in MavlinkConsole to avoid 'closed before established' warning on rapid nav.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py L143: MAVLink v2 build assumes msgid is in CRC_EXTRA — good; the /craft endpoint already 400s otherwise. Confirmed correct behavior in tests.
backend/server.py: All handlers use async/await + Motor. MongoDB _id is excluded on every read via projection. No sync blocking.
backend/server.py L578: CORS uses '*' from env. For a defense app this is intentionally permissive for eval; should be tightened before any production deployment.
frontend/lib/api.js: axios base URL comes from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — correct. Token added via interceptor from localStorage.
auth: bcrypt is used with $2b$ hash format (imported bcrypt, hashpw+gensalt). Seed_admin is idempotent AND refreshes hash if admin password changed. Good.

No brute-force lockout implemented. Not in the review scope (test_credentials.md does not require it) but is called out in the auth playbook — consider adding rate limiting on /api/auth/login before production.
WebSocket auth via ?token= query parameter is fine for JWT bearer style; unauthenticated connections are closed with 1008 (verified).
seed_data_creation: None manual. Backend auto-seeds 6 detections on startup + admin user. Tests further create ~10 detections during their run (all prefixed via random callsigns) and neutralize them via PL-005/PL-010; state is left in a NEUTRALIZED-heavy mode for the operator UI but /simulate can re-populate active contacts at any time.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite is at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py — run with `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_backend.py -v -o asyncio_mode=auto --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (falls back to reading /app/frontend/.env). Note the parent /app/backend/pytest.ini enforces xdist -n 2 --dist loadscope; tests are designed to be class-safe. If PL-010 broadcast leaves no active targets, run /api/detections/simulate before frontend Payloads tests that require an active target-select option.
rca of the issue: No unresolved bugs. The single frontend test-side hiccup ('log-* rows = 0') was a timing issue in the first playwright pass — Mission Log renders the container immediately but populates rows after the api.get('/logs') resolves; re-running with wait_for_selector('[data-testid^="log-"]') showed 25 log rows spanning all expected kinds (AUTH/DETECTION/MAVLINK/PAYLOAD/CEMA/KILLCHAIN/UPLOAD). App is functionally correct.
